name: ZyVersa Therapeutics, Inc.
business: ZyVersa Therapeutics is a clinical stage biopharmaceutical company developing drugs for patients with chronic renal or inflammatory diseases. It has two proprietary drug platforms: Cholesterol Efflux Mediator VAR 200 (for renal diseases, lead indication FSGS) and Inflammasome ASC Inhibitor IC 100 (for inflammatory diseases, lead indication cardiometabolic conditions associated with obesity). The company has not generated any revenue from product sales.

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical stage biopharmaceutical company leveraging proprietary technologies to develop drugs for patients with chronic renal or inflammatory diseases with high unmet medical needs.

catalysts.event: Initiate Phase 2a basket trial in FSGS and Alport syndrome patients
catalysts.sentence: We plan to initiate a Phase 2a basket trial in FSGS and Alport syndrome patients in Q2-2026.

Our focus is on advancing IC 100 toward a currently planned IND submission in Q4-2026, followed by initiation of a Phase 1 trial in healthy overweight patients with a BMI between 27 30 at risk for cardiometabolic conditions.

catalysts.event: Initiate IND-enabling preclinical studies for IC 100
catalysts.sentence: We are preparing to initiate IND-enabling preclinical studies in an animal model of diet-induced obesity, which develops metabolic complications, and an animal model representative of an orphan renal disease in Q2-2026.

tamClaims.claim: Global renal drug market was $20 billion in 2024 and projected to reach $30 billion by 2034.
tamClaims.sentence: According to a report from Precedence Research, the global renal drug market was $20 billion in 2024 and projected to reach $30 billion by 2034.

tamClaims.claim: Global anti-inflammatory biologics market was $104.81 billion in 2024 and projected to reach $185.51 billion by 2034.
tamClaims.sentence: According to a report from Precedence Research, the global anti-inflammatory biologics market was valued at $104.81 billion in 2024, and it is projected to reach $185.51 billion by 2034.
